skill.name: remotion-render
skill.description: Render videos from React/Remotion component code via inference.sh. Pass TSX code, get MP4. Supports all Remotion APIs: useCurrentFrame, useVideoConfig, spring, interpolate, AbsoluteFill, Sequence. Configurable resolution, FPS, duration, codec.
skill.summary: Generate MP4 videos from React component code using Remotion and the inference.sh cloud rendering platform.
skill.author: skills-shell

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 41 findings: 32 external_commands (backtick execution in markdown code blocks), 6 network (documentation URLs), and 3 high-severity weak-crypto patterns (false matches on YAML frontmatter and markdown tables). After evaluation, all 41 findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The SKILL.md file is pure documentation with CLI usage examples in code blocks and standard hyperlinks. No executable code, no cryptographic operations, no actual command execution occurs from this markdown file. The skill is safe to publish.

content.user_title: Generate MP4 Videos from React Component Code
content.value_statement: Creating programmatic videos from React code requires complex rendering pipelines. This skill lets Claude generate Remotion component code and render it to MP4 video via the inference.sh cloud platform.
actual_capabilities: Generate Remotion React component code for video rendering
Configure video parameters including resolution, FPS, duration, and codec
Create animations using Remotion APIs like spring, interpolate, and Sequence
Render TSX code to MP4 video files via the belt CLI
Pass props to video components for data-driven content
Stream rendering progress updates via the Python SDK
limitations: Requires an inference.sh account and belt CLI installation
Video rendering happens on remote servers, not locally on your machine
Limited to Remotion and React imports available in the cloud environment
Maximum video duration and complexity depend on inference.sh plan limits

use_cases.title: Generate Social Media Video Content
use_cases.description: Create animated promo videos, title sequences, and social media clips from React component code without video editing software.

use_cases.title: Create Data-Driven Visualizations
use_cases.description: Turn data into animated charts and visualizations by passing data as props to Remotion components rendered as video.

use_cases.title: Automate Video Production Workflows
use_cases.description: Batch generate programmatic videos with different text, colors, and animations by scripting component props.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Fade-In Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a simple Remotion video that shows Hello World fading in over 3 seconds at 1080p resolution.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with your first programmatic video

prompt_templates.title: Spring-Animated Element
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a Remotion component with a spring-animated logo that bounces in from the left side of the screen. Use useCurrentFrame and spring APIs.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding dynamic motion to video elements

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Scene Sequence
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a multi-scene video with Sequence components showing an intro scene, two content scenes, and an outro. Each scene should have fade transitions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building videos with multiple timed sections

prompt_templates.title: Data-Driven Animated Charts
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a data-driven Remotion video that accepts an array of chart data as props and renders animated bar charts with interpolated heights and labels for each bar.

output_examples.input: Create a countdown video from 10 to 1 with large white text on black background
output_examples.output: Generates a Remotion component using useCurrentFrame to display decreasing numbers at 1-second intervals. The belt CLI renders it as a 10-second 1080p MP4 with smooth number transitions.

output_examples.input: Make a product showcase video with spring-animated product cards appearing one by one
output_examples.output: Creates a component with Sequence and spring animations. Each product card scales up with a bounce effect, staggered by 30 frames. Outputs a configurable-length MP4.

best_practices: Start with simple single-component videos before building complex multi-scene animations
Test Remotion component logic locally with Remotion Studio before sending to the cloud renderer
Use composition_id and props for reusable video templates instead of hardcoding values into components
anti_patterns: Do not include side effects or async data fetching inside Remotion components as they run every frame
Avoid embedding sensitive API keys or credentials in component code sent to the cloud renderer
Do not assume local file system access as the cloud environment has no access to your files

faq.question: What is Remotion?
faq.answer: Remotion is a React-based framework for creating videos programmatically using React components and familiar web technologies.

faq.question: Do I need to install Remotion locally?
faq.answer: No, the cloud renderer handles all Remotion dependencies. You only need the belt CLI to submit render jobs.

faq.question: What video formats are supported?
faq.answer: The renderer outputs MP4 video files. You can configure resolution, frame rate, duration, and codec in the input parameters.

faq.question: Can I use custom fonts and images?
faq.answer: Custom fonts and images depend on what is available in the inference.sh cloud environment. Check their documentation for supported assets.

faq.question: How long does rendering take?
faq.answer: Rendering time depends on video duration, resolution, and complexity. Most short videos complete in under one minute.

faq.question: Is this skill free to use?
faq.answer: The skill itself is free, but rendering videos requires an inference.sh account which may have usage limits or associated costs.
